name: Tajikistan
avoid: October–May for the Pamir Highway unless you are prepared for snow, landslides and sudden road closures
Starting long shared-taxi days after lunch — mountain roads are safer and smoother in daylight
Leaving GBAO paperwork to the last minute if you want Khorog or the wider Pamir route

cities.name: Dushanbe
cities.notes: Main arrival city for museums, visa errands, Hissar Fortress and the easiest place to sort SIMs, cash and Pamir permits before heading out

cities.name: Pamir Highway (Khorog)
cities.notes: Use Khorog as the Pamir base for the botanical garden, bazaar, Gunt River walks and onward 4x4 trips deeper into GBAO

cities.name: Iskanderkul Lake
cities.notes: Classic alpine stop for lakeshore views, Fann Niagara and a simple homestay night in Sarytag or by the water

cities.name: Penjikent
cities.notes: Best cultural base for Ancient Penjikent, Sarazm and Seven Lakes or Camp Artuch outings in the Fann Mountains

cities.name: Khujand
cities.notes: Sughd-region city on the Syr Darya known for Panjshanbe Bazaar, the cable car and a softer Silk Road finale

itinerary.sim: Tcell or MegaFon tourist SIMs are easiest in Dushanbe; expect roughly ₹300–₹700 for enough data for a 7–10 day trip, and download offline maps before the Pamir Highway.

Dushanbe.name: Dushanbe
Dushanbe.note: Two days covers the city highlights plus Hissar Fortress; keep a third only for permit buffer, galleries or a slower pre-Pamir reset.

days.theme: Rudaki Park, National Museum & Classic Dushanbe

activities.name: Rudaki Park, Ismoil Somoni Monument and flagpole walk
National Museum of Tajikistan
Mehrgon or Green Bazaar snack-and-fruit stop
Yandex Go taxi hops around the centre
Dinner at Rokhat Teahouse or Traktir
Shared taxi or arranged cab to Hissar Fortress and old madrasa complex
Hissar Fortress entry
activities.tip: Official entry is 10 TJS, so keep a few small somoni notes handy.

activities.name: Noor Art Gallery or Botanical Garden back in Dushanbe
GBAO permit printing / visa admin before the Pamir leg
activities.tip: The standard GBAO permit is about $20 online; carry multiple printed copies for checkpoints.

days.theme: Optional Buffer Before a Flight or Shared Taxi

activities.name: Hotel rest, laundry and ATM run before the Pamir or northbound road leg
Early airport transfer for a Tajik Air domestic flight or dawn shared taxi departure

Pamir Highway (Khorog).name: Pamir Highway (Khorog)
Pamir Highway (Khorog).note: Three days gives Khorog a proper look plus one long 4x4 outing; add days only if you want to continue toward the Wakhan or Bulunkul lakes.

days.theme: Arrival in Khorog & Gunt River Strolls

activities.name: Tajik Air domestic flight from Dushanbe to Khorog when weather and schedule permit
activities.tip: If flights are cancelled, fall back to a shared jeep or private 4x4 on the Pamir Highway.

activities.name: Khorog City Park and bazaar walk
Dinner at Lal Hotel Restaurant or your guesthouse
Khorog Botanical Garden
Regional Museum of Khorog
Shared taxi up to the botanical garden ridge and back
Afghan Market or bazaar snacks if your dates line up
Shared 4x4 hire from Khorog toward the Wakhan Valley or Jelondy hot springs corridor
Checkpoint copies for GBAO permit
activities.tip: Most drivers expect you to hand over photocopies at checkpoints, so keep spares from Dushanbe.

activities.name: Picnic lunch and tea stops in village chaikhanas

days.theme: Extra Day for Bulunkul, Wakhan or Weather

activities.name: Reserve this day if flights are delayed or you want to keep driving farther along the Pamir Highway
Optional overnight jeep extension or a recovery day back in Khorog

Iskanderkul Lake.name: Iskanderkul Lake
Iskanderkul Lake.note: One night is enough for lake views and a short waterfall walk; only add a second if you want a slower Sarytag hike or a weather cushion.

activities.name: Shared taxi arrival via Varzob and the Anzob route
Iskanderkul lakeshore trail and viewpoint stops
Short hike to Fann Niagara waterfall
Guesthouse set dinner by the lake
Short village walk or easy valley hike from Sarytag before moving on
Shared taxi onward toward Ayni or Penjikent

Penjikent.name: Penjikent
Penjikent.note: Two days works well for one history day and one Fann Mountains outing; keep a third only for Sarazm plus Panjrud or a slower trek start.

activities.name: Ancient Penjikent ruins
Rudaki Museum
Sarazm Archaeological Site and museum shelter area
Panjakent Bazaar tea-and-bread stop
Shared taxi to Haft-Kul (Seven Lakes) or Camp Artuch trailhead
Fann Mountains trekking access / campsite fee at Camp Artuch or village guest stop
activities.tip: There is no formal government trekking permit for the main Fann routes; this is the usual local camp or trailhead fee travelers actually pay.

activities.name: Simple lakeside lunch or camp tea
Drive to Panjrud for the Rudaki Mausoleum if you want an extra literary stop

Khujand.name: Khujand
Khujand.note: One day gets the fortress, bazaar and cable car done; a second day helps if you want Arbob Palace and a more relaxed Syr Darya evening.

activities.name: Khujand Fortress and Local History Museum
Panjshanbe Bazaar food walk
Khujand cable car round trip over the Syr Darya
activities.tip: Round-trip fare is about 25 TJS; one-way tickets are cheaper if you plan to walk back.

activities.name: Kamoli Khujandi Park sunset stroll
Arbob Cultural Palace
Somoni Park and riverside pause
Taxi back to the station or airport for departure

connections.method: Tajik Air domestic flight when it operates; otherwise take a shared jeep or pre-booked 4x4 hire via Kalaikhum on the Pamir Highway (14–16 hr by road)
Most travelers return to Dushanbe by 4x4 and continue north the next morning by shared taxi to Sarytag or the lake area
Shared taxi via Ayni or onward seat in a northbound car through the Fann corridor (about 2.5–3.5 hr)
Shared taxi along the Zarafshon valley road (about 5–6 hr)

extras: Best weather is usually June–September; this is when the Pamir Highway, Iskanderkul and Seven Lakes are easiest to combine in one loop.
Shared taxis are the default transport across Tajikistan and usually leave only when full, so start early and keep your day flexible.
Carry enough cash before the Pamir leg — cards and ATMs are unreliable outside Dushanbe and major Khujand hotels.
If the Tajik Air Khorog flight is cancelled, a driver + 4x4 hire is the normal fallback; do not count on same-day rebooking.
For a shorter 7-day trip, keep Dushanbe, Iskanderkul, Penjikent and Khujand and save the Pamir Highway for a dedicated return trip.
